kill_fact: The load-bearing event is a SUPPLY RELEASE with no contractual payout — bearish-on-arrival, not a value-resolving close. The 8-K (2025-08-30) is confirmed verbatim (17.5%/20%/27.5% tranches done, final ~77.7M sh / ~35% on the earlier of Q2'26 earnings+2d or 2026-08-31), so the date is real, but the $32 target is PURE analyst-multiple hope with zero event-locked floor. Decisive disqualifier vs the bull's 'absorb-then-re-rate' mechanism: the three prior tranches already cleared across Q3'25-Q1'26 and did NOT produce a re-rate — FIG fell ~40% YTD and sits at/near its $16.60 52-wk low THROUGH the absorption, empirically falsifying the claim that exhausting the seller repaces the float. The final >$6B tranche is the largest and lowest-basis, so the single dated catalyst can pin or pierce the floor for weeks. This is a watchlist-for-capitulation (B), never a front-run.
corrections: (1) GAAP optics overstated/stale: dossier cites net loss -$1.40B and EPS -$3.38. Primary filings show FY2025 GAAP net loss ~$(1.3)B (driven by a one-time $975.7M IPO stock-comp charge in Q3'25) and Q1'26 net loss only -$142.4M / EPS -$(0.27) with non-GAAP op margin +16% and +$89M FCF. The -$3.38 figure conflates the one-time IPO-SBC window; the business is non-GAAP profitable and FCF-positive, so the 'money-loser keeps generalists away' point is weaker than the dossier frames. (2) The downside 'floor' (~$17.50 / $16.60 low) is a multiple-on-growth technical floor, NOT net cash — confirmed it can and nearly did pierce during prior absorption; treat as soft, not bounded. (3) Growth/valuation anchors verified: Q1'26 +46% to $333.4M, NDR 139%, FY guide $1.422-1.428B (~35% midpoint => decelerating vs 46% spot); EV/S ~7x and ~$36.88 street avg (Hold, 3 buy / 7 hold) all confirmed.
evidence: Figma 8-K filed 2025-08-30 (Extended Lock-Up, 54.1% of Class A): tranches 17.5% (~38.9M) post-Q3'25, 20% (~44.4M) post-FY25, 27.5% (~61.1M) post-Q1'26, remainder ~77.7M on earlier of Q2'26 earnings+2 trading days or 2026-08-31 — confirms catalyst is real, dated, binding, supply-RELEASE.

Figma Q1'26 results (Businesswire / 8-K press release, 2026-05-14): revenue $333.4M +46% YoY, NDR 139%, FY26 guide $1.422-1.428B (~35% midpoint), Q1'26 GAAP net loss -$142.4M / EPS -$(0.27), non-GAAP op margin 16%, FCF +$89M — corrects the -$1.40B/-$3.38 loss optics.
FY2025 10-K / 8-K: GAAP net loss ~$(1.3)B including one-time $975.7M IPO stock-comp charge in Q3'25 — the billion-dollar loss is an IPO-mechanics artifact, not ongoing cash burn.

Live quote 2026-06-21/22 (Yahoo/CNBC): ~$18.94-19.18 (confirms ~$19.12), 52-wk range $16.60-$142.92, street avg target $36.88, consensus Hold (3 buy/7 hold) — confirms price, washout, and that upside is analyst-multiple, not contractual.

Floor-pierce confirmation (stockanalysis.com/stocks/fig, stockstotrade.com FIG 2026-06-25/26): FIG closed $16.84 on 2026-06-25 — BELOW the stated $17.50 floor, empirically proving the floor is soft — then bounced to $18.62 on 2026-06-26 on Citi Buy/$36 initiation (2026-06-17) + activist Findell news; the bounce does not de-risk the final Aug-31 supply tranche still ahead.
